title: Eleven Discourses on Māra, Etc.
text:
## 3. Appeals

At Sāvatthī. “You should give up any desire, any greed, any desire and greed for whatever is subject to Māra …” “… impermanent …” “… liable to impermanence …” “… suffering …” “… liable to suffering …” “… not-self …” “… liable to not-self …” “… liable to end …” “… liable to fall …” “… liable to originate …” Seated to one side, Venerable Rādha said to the Buddha: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”

“Rādha, you should give up any desire, any greed, any desire and greed for whatever is Māra. And what is Māra? Form is Māra. You should give up any desire, any greed, any desire and greed for it. Feeling … Perception … Choices … Consciousness is Māra. You should give up any desire, any greed, any desire and greed for it. You should give up any desire, any greed, any desire and greed for whatever is Māra.”
